nodes.label: The metered 80/20 — GD-share 0.121
nodes.rationale: The first time the founding '80% cheap unsupervised structure + 20% precise naming' thesis is a measured hardware energy number rather than an op-count proxy: with the committed DDM gate on, the GD namer is 12.1% of total metered energy (≤0.25); off (always-pay) it balloons to 77.8% — the gate itself creates the split. OURS also draws ~half (bp_ratio 0.501) the energy of a fair BP+replay learner at matched retention on the same substrate table.

nodes.label: BP+replay energy model — the fair conventional baseline
nodes.rationale: A backward-pass-every-step plus a replay mini-batch sized to reach OURS's retention, metered on the identical per-op energy table — deliberately not a naive backward-every-step BP that forgets (a strawman), so bp_ratio is a fair energy fight, though the fair same-budget accuracy fight remains owed to Phase 9/10.
